_publications._publicationTitle: Transient inhibition of the ERK pathway prevents cerebellar developmental defects and improves long-term motor functions in murine models of neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: Rapamycin treatment had no benefit on neuronal defects in the Nf1 Math1 CKO cerebellum, but caused adverse effects on neuronal migration in both the control and mutant cerebellum
details: Treatment had no benefit on neuronal defects in Nf1 Math1 CKO cerebellum, but caused adverse effects on neuronal migration in both control and mutant cerebellum
